# Pinpoint

> Pinpoint is CodePal's open-source policy gateway for sensitive MCP data. It wraps an unmodified stdio MCP server, executes operator-approved exact projections into hidden destination tools without placing values in model context, and returns signed disclosure-bounded receipts. It also retains oversized results locally for bounded exact queries.

## Start here

- [README](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/README.md): enterprise business case, target owners, evaluation path, deployment fit, evidence, quickstart, and technical appendix
- [Security policy](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/SECURITY.md): deployment trust boundaries, private reporting, and claims Pinpoint does not make
- [Value-opaque MCP dataflow](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/planning/value_opaque_mcp_dataflow.md): flow policy, fail-closed source capture, random capabilities, signed receipts, evidence, prior art, and remaining security gates
- [Opaque-flow formal properties](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/planning/opaque_flow_formal_properties.md): Spin reference model, checked invariants, mutation sensitivity, implementation mapping, and explicit non-proofs
- [Breakthrough scorecard](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/planning/breakthrough_scorecard.md): strict local/external gates and the current not-yet-proven verdict
- [Benchmark report](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/benchmarks/REPORT.md): generated evidence split by simulation, offline transform, live controlled, and live agent levels
- [Reproducing benchmarks](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/benchmarks/REPRODUCING.md): no-key checks and capped paid replication workflow
- [QCV design](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/planning/query_backed_context.md): exact-operation boundaries, threat model, rejected design, and prior art
- [Product assessment](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/planning/product_assessment.md): evidence limits, competitive position, validation gates, and kill criteria
- [MCP result firewall design](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/planning/mcp_result_firewall.md): upstream interception, exact artifact protocol, invariants, limits, and threat model
- [OSS adoption research](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/planning/oss_adoption_research.md): source-backed README and launch patterns from high-attention AI developer projects
- [Public launch checklist](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/planning/public_launch.md): manual evidence, media, npm, GitHub, community, and launch-sequence gates

## Primary product path

- [CLI entrypoint](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/src/cli/main.ts): `mcp gateway`, standalone `mcp`, `demo`, `proxy`, `wrap`, `doctor`, `stats`, `replay`, `integration`, and `agent`
- [MCP gateway](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/src/mcp/gateway.ts): transparent stdio JSON-RPC forwarding, exact result virtualization, resources, `pinpoint_query`, and internal opaque destination dispatch
- [Opaque flow engine](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/src/mcp/flow.ts): versioned policy validation, exact projection confinement, HMAC commitments, Ed25519 receipts, chaining, and verification
- [Flow policy example](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/examples/mcp-opaque-flow.json): source, destination, filter, projection, fixed arguments, and limits
- [Flow policy schema](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/examples/mcp-opaque-flow.schema.json): editor and deployment-tooling schema; runtime validation remains authoritative
- [Private destination example](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/examples/mcp-opaque-destination.json): separate stdio process, environment allowlist, and explicit shared-name subset
- [Anthropic SDK wrapper](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/src/sdk/anthropic.ts): wrap the official Anthropic TypeScript client
- [OpenAI SDK wrapper](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/src/sdk/openai.ts): wrap Chat Completions and Responses clients
- [Integration examples](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/tree/main/examples/integrations): public integration API examples with built-ins disabled
- [Configuration](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/src/config.ts): typed runtime settings and environment variables

## Trust and contribution

- [Raw paid QCV receipt](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/benchmarks/results/direct-anthropic-virtual.json): two-task Haiku 4.5 pilot with usage, outputs, costs, order, and limitations
- [Real-agent MCP gateway receipt](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/benchmarks/results/mcp-gateway-agent.first-party-macos-arm64-20260715.json): one synthetic Claude Code tool-to-artifact-to-query flow with source fingerprints and explicit limitations
- [Cross-host MCP gateway receipt](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/benchmarks/results/mcp-gateway-cross-host.first-party-macos-arm64-20260715.json): the same exact task passed on Claude Code and GitHub Copilot CLI with a shared artifact id; Cursor and Codex blocks are explicitly uncounted
- [Opaque-flow protocol receipt](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/benchmarks/results/mcp-opaque-flow.first-party-macos-arm64-20260715.json): 30 exact hidden calls, eight denied bypasses, zero of 400 canaries leaked, operator/session/policy authorization, signed chain verification, and local latency
- [Opaque-flow cross-host receipt](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/benchmarks/results/mcp-opaque-flow-cross-host.first-party-macos-arm64-20260715.json): Claude Code and Copilot each completed the same hidden destination flow with valid receipts and zero event-stream fixture values
- [Opaque-flow model-check receipt](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/benchmarks/results/opaque-flow-model-check.first-party-macos-arm64-20260715.json): 2,270,040 states and 3,416,444 transitions, zero assertion violations, deliberate value-leak and credential-copy mutations detected
- [Published filesystem MCP receipt](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/benchmarks/results/mcp-oss-filesystem.first-party-macos-arm64-20260715.json): unmodified pinned OSS server, exact row recovery, 999 unrelated canaries absent
- [Published cross-server MCP receipt](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/benchmarks/results/mcp-oss-cross-server.first-party-macos-arm64-20260716.json): unmodified filesystem -> memory servers, exact 40-entity side effect, 4/4 denials, 0/600 canaries, separate process environments
- [Matched HCP comparison receipt](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/benchmarks/results/hcp-comparison.first-party-macos-arm64-20260716.json): byte-identical workflow; Pinpoint exact, HCP 30/30 exact, both 4/4 native denials and 0/600 canaries; no scalar winner
- [Contributing](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): architecture contract, evidence labels, tests, and release process
- [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md): community expectations, private reporting, and enforcement
- [Upstream attribution](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/UPSTREAM.md): pinned pxpipe and Headroom contracts
- [Changelog](https://github.com/CodePalAI/pinpoint/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md): release history

## Important boundaries

- Best fit: a local or VPC-side wrapper around an existing stdio MCP server, owned jointly by AI platform and security engineering.
- The MCP gateway is the primary path and does not require provider API-key interception. It handles eligible successful text and structured MCP results before host truncation.
- Loading a flow policy changes configured sources from optimization/fail-open semantics to confidentiality/fail-closed semantics at every result size. Query/resources are hidden and artifact ids are random by default.
- Opaque-flow receipts hide values, not metadata: tool/field names, operation, counts, sizes, timing, policy limits, and success remain visible. The wrapped process is trusted with values.
- Pinpoint returns flow receipts through MCP but does not persist them. Durable receipt retention belongs in the host or an existing collector; body capture may contain sensitive values.
- Errors, media, mixed content, ambiguous nested collections, small results, and capacity failures pass through unchanged.
- Gateway artifacts are bounded, process-scoped, and queryable only through capped exact operations or bounded resource previews.
- Provider-wire QCV remains a secondary path for supported operations over older JSON, logs, and source tool output. Ambiguous or unsupported requests pass through.
- The model-planned fallback is experimental and disabled by default.
- OAuth and subscription traffic keeps provider-wire QCV off, but can use the MCP gateway because it operates before provider authentication.
- "Local" describes optimization and temporary dataset storage. Normal model requests still go to the provider selected by the user.
- The cross-host MCP receipt is one first-party synthetic task replicated across two clients, not a prevalence estimate. The 96.8% provider-wire result used deliberately eligible synthetic prompts, not normal coding traffic.
- Optional operator-rooted sessions authenticate a configured Ed25519 key and exact hidden policy commitment; they do not prove the key's organizational identity or detect omitted sessions without external witnesses.
- One separate private stdio destination is supported with an independent catalog/request namespace and destination-exclusive environment names. This is not OS sandboxing or remote OAuth brokering.
- Closest runnable prior art comparison: HCP is stronger on principal/grant/resource/approval semantics and rich audit; Pinpoint is stronger on unmodified MCP interoperability, exact row/field policy, process separation, and signed receipts. Independent comparison remains open.
- Not yet supported: multiple/remote destinations, externally witnessed identity/transparency, remote attestation, exactly-once side effects, or formal compliance claims.
